channel.title: Jn-Huang's Daily Briefings
channel.description: AI, tech, science, finance, and world news — quick summaries powered by Claude

item.title: Fed Holds Rates at 3.5-3.75%, Projects Only One Cut in 2026 Amid Elevated Uncertainty
item.description:
The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate steady at 3.5% to 3.75% at its March 17-18 meeting — the second consecutive pause. The decision was nearly unanimous, with one dissent from Governor Stephen Miran, who pushed for a quarter-point cut.

The FOMC's updated projections tell the story: GDP growth for 2026 was nudged up to 2.4% (from 2.3% in December), but core PCE inflation was revised higher to 2.7% (from 2.5%). The median dot plot still shows just one rate cut this year — a hawkish hold that reflects the Fed's struggle to reconcile solid growth with sticky inflation.

The statement flagged "elevated uncertainty" about the economic outlook, pointing specifically to the Middle East conflict as a wild card for the U.S. economy. With Brent crude recently hovering near $100 on shifting ceasefire talk headlines, energy prices remain a key inflation variable the Fed can't control.

Markets have largely adjusted. Expectations for 2026 rate cuts have evaporated in the U.S., and European counterparts (ECB, Bank of England) are now leaning toward hikes. The S&P 500 added 1.2% on March 25 as traders parsed US-Iran diplomatic signals, while 10-year Treasury yields edged down 3 basis points to 4.33%.

Next data point to watch: February durable goods orders, due out today, which will signal whether manufacturing is holding up under the current rate environment.

Sources:

  • https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260318a.htm
  • https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomcprojtabl20260318.htm
  • https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/stock-market-today-dow-s-p-live-updates
